segments.text: Welcome everybody to the presentation which covers workflows and apps on other disk with using Victor.
Together with Luca Ovalien, the CEO of Swaco, I'll be presenting.
I'm Peter Matliner, the CEO of Victor.
We will be presenting multiple things in this presentation, but take about 30 minutes.
You will learn how to turn an engineer logic into reusable apps in minutes instead of weeks,
how to connect out-to-description clouds and other tools into a single automation workflow,
and you will learn how AI agents can orchestrate engineering processes not just automate individual tasks.
Before we introduce Victor, let's talk about the challenges that we have in the engineering industry.
We have a housing shortage, we have aging infrastructure, we have engineering transition,
we have challenges regarding climate.
This is by the way a very interesting topic that Luca will also be presenting the things that they are building in this domain.
But there is another challenge, we have a housing shortage, etc.
We have the challenge of the engineer in the context that the engineer is working.
There is a budget pressure, there is time shortage, and there is a shortage in talent.
Other industries are already heavily automating their work,
and the automation is of course an opportunity to help the engineer to solve those challenges that the industry has.
Looking at different industries like media, finance, utilities, healthcare, they are way ahead regarding automation,
and engineering is staying behind.
But this is changing, and that's why we also are presenting about this.
AI is a real strong enabler for engineers to start automating.
But at the moment, this is a chart that is being made by anthropocon recently published,
there is a huge potential for engineers and automation for AI.
It's a very little adopted industry, technology in the industry.
So what enables AI for the engineer, it enables the democratization or software creation.
So everybody, by means of AI, everybody can start making automation tools.
And it's really important for the industry, engineering companies, construction companies to implement this technology,
because if you don't do so, you will fall behind, and that will be a disadvantage when it comes down to competitiveness.
So looking at our customers, what are the main challenges in automation currently?
So we have five challenges.
So the engineers' in coding skills, automation by itself takes too much time.
It's a fragmented data software landscape.
So the engineers are very different, and there's always a very high quality standard required.
So where do we help with Victor and these?
So we make sure any engineer can build apps by means of AI.
We make sure that the engineers can build those apps super fast, such that it fits in their way of working.
So we then they're existing landscape that you make apps that are being verified, that you know that the quality is of the highest standard.
And eventually you can share those verified apps with your colleagues.
And how we categorize this is build, connect and share.
And these are also the pillars that we have within Victor.
Okay, so that was briefly about Victor in introduction.
I will be giving a live demo on how we are using these elements to connect different applications and orchestrate workflows.
But before doing so, I want to play the floor to Luca.
Thank you, Peter.
So to start with, I will shortly introduce this record.

We have 22,000 people and offices in 15 countries, and we are providing different services in our landscape from the building and urban areas, industry, infrastructure, and texture, but also sustainability and resilience.
So we have a very big true digital strategy that is based on these five digital pillars.
One of the most important part of our work is of course to provide those services and we want to provide those services with most efficient and qualitative way possible.
We also want to transform the cloud experience that using digital tools and technologies to really provide additional values in our projects.
Also, we want to start unloading new business and new business models.
We want to fill the gap on the market that we are seeing daily.
And also then two fundamental pillars that we want to build continue building our digital ag capabilities also are innovative mindset.
And then the last is of course digitalizing the back office so that we keep improving our existing operational processes.

If you really improve and build more internal automations that we start building the small and shared applications across the teams across the units and across divisions.

We can also speed up delivery of these applications to the end users that can actually start using those and get some efficiencies and some value on their side.
